scenario: Magnolia Retail, a retailer, wants to migrate 700 instances of its Camunda 8.8 returns-handling process. Service task A inside Subprocess A must move to service task B inside Subprocess B in the new model. The team is writing the migration plan and wants to know which mapping instructions are required.
question: Which mapping instructions are required to migrate the active service task located inside the subprocess?

options.text: A mapping instruction from element id A to element id B, plus a mapping instruction from subprocess id A to subprocess id B, both provided in the same migration plan for the running instance.
A mapping instruction from element id A to element id B only; the engine derives the subprocess mapping automatically from the BPMN parent relationship between source and target elements.
A mapping instruction from subprocess id A to subprocess id B only; the engine then migrates the contained active service task by matching its source id to a target id of the same shape.
A mapping instruction from element id A to element id B and a parent mapping from subprocess id A to an event subprocess id B; the scope must broaden during migration of active elements.

a.text: Correct. To migrate an active element inside a subprocess, the migration plan must contain mappings for both the inner element and the containing subprocess, here A to B and Subprocess A to Subprocess B.

b.text: Incorrect. The engine does not derive subprocess mappings from BPMN parentage; the migration plan must explicitly map the source subprocess id to the target subprocess id.

c.text: Incorrect. Mapping only the subprocess does not move the active inner element to its new id; both the element and subprocess mappings are required.

d.text: Incorrect. You cannot migrate an active embedded subprocess to an event subprocess; the scope cannot change in this way during process instance migration.

explanation: Migrating an active element inside a subprocess requires both an element-to-element mapping and a subprocess-to-subprocess mapping in the migration plan so the engine can move the inner active step onto its new container.
